name: Nuevo Baztán and Tajuña Greenway Loop
name_original: Río Tajuña – circular de Nuevo Baztán a Mondéjar – gravel
description: This difficult 21.4-mile (34.4 km) gravel cycling route from Nuevo Baztán to Mondéjar offers challenging terrain and diverse scenery.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Nuevo Baztán offers routes through the Alcarria landscape, characterized by holm-oaks, gall-oaks, and agricultural lands. The region features river valleys such as Valle del Tajuña and Arroyo Vega, providing scenic paths for cyclists. Elevation changes are present, with routes winding through picturesque hills and valleys, offering diverse terrain for touring cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Nuevo Baztán The most popular touring cycling route is Nuevo Baztán and Tajuña Greenway Loop, a 21.4 miles (34.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 40 minutes to complete, featuring diverse scenery along a former railway line. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Plaza de Santorcaz – Abánades Entrance loop from Pezuela de las Torres, a difficult 36.1 miles (58.1 km) path. This route traverses varied landscapes, including open fields and rural roads. Local touring cyclists also love the Eiffel Tower – Bridge Over the Henares River loop from Ronda Hispanoamericana - Ronda Manchester, a 44.9 miles (72.2 km) trail leading through river valleys and agricultural areas, often completed in about 5 hours 50 minutes. Touring cycling around Nuevo Baztán is defined by the Alcarria landscape, river valleys, and historic towns. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Nuevo Baztán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Nuevo Baztán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Nuevo Baztán?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes around Nuevo Baztán, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 20 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Nuevo Baztán?
A: The terrain around Nuevo Baztán is characterized by the Alcarria landscape, featuring picturesque hills, valleys, and agricultural lands with holm-oaks and gall-oaks. You'll find routes winding through river valleys like the Valle del Tajuña and Arroyo Vega, as well as former railway lines adapted for cycling, known as Vías Verdes. Expect a mix of paved and gravel surfaces, with varying elevation changes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961425137/nuevo-baztan-and-tajuna-greenway-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nuevo Baztán and Tajuña Greenway Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 34.4 km trail that offers diverse scenery along a former railway line. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31921945" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Santorcaz – Abánades Entrance loop from Pezuela de las Torres&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 58.1 km through varied landscapes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or historical sites I can see while cycling around Nuevo Baztán?
A: Nuevo Baztán itself is a Historic-Artistic Monument. You can visit the Baroque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3045704" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Goyeneche Palace of Nuevo Baztán&lt;/a&gt; and the Church of San Francisco Javier. Along routes, you might encounter historical fountains like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3501590" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valmores Fountain&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3031028" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuente Grande de Corpa&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features ancient cattle trails like the Cañada Real Galiana and scenic viewpoints such as Mirador de la Encina.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes suitable for children?
A: While many routes in the area feature moderate to difficult grades, there are some easier options. The Vías Verdes (Greenways), such as sections of the Vía Verde del Tajuña, are generally flatter and more suitable for families. Look for routes described as 'easy' or 'moderate' on komoot, which typically have less elevation gain and smoother surfaces.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling trails in Nuevo Baztán?
A: Many natural paths and greenways in the region, including sections of the Vía Verde del Tajuña, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other cyclists and pedestrians. Check local signage for any specific restrictions on certain trails.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Nuevo Baztán?
A: The spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months are generally ideal for touring cycling in Nuevo Baztán. During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter conditions of winter. Spring also offers the beauty of blooming landscapes.

Q: Are there options for more challenging touring cycling routes in Nuevo Baztán?
A: Absolutely. Nuevo Baztán offers 21 difficult touring cycling routes for experienced riders. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and cover longer distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40390769" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eiffel Tower – Bridge Over the Henares River loop from Ronda Hispanoamericana - Ronda Manchester&lt;/a&gt;, a 72.2 km trail with considerable climbing, often completed in about 5 hours 50 minutes.

Q: Where can I find parking if I'm bringing my bike to Nuevo Baztán?
A: Nuevo Baztán, as a historic town and a hub for outdoor activities, generally offers parking facilities within or near the town center. Many routes start from the town, making it convenient to park your vehicle. Look for designated public parking areas upon arrival. For specific trailheads outside the town, check the route details on komoot for parking information.

Q: What natural features can I explore while cycling in the region?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through the Valle del Tajuña and Arroyo Vega, which offer scenic paths. The Alcarria landscape is characterized by its holm-oaks and gall-oaks. Don't miss the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7267547" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valhondo Valley&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7084139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Geology of the Tajuña Valley Landscape&lt;/a&gt; for impressive geological formations.

Q: Are there any routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Yes, several routes provide excellent viewpoints. The region's diverse landscapes, including hills and valleys, naturally lend themselves to scenic vistas. The route from Alcalá de Henares to Nuevo Baztán, for instance, is known for panoramic views of mountains and valleys. Keep an eye out for specific viewpoints like Mirador de la Encina along your chosen path.

Q: Can I access touring cycling routes in Nuevo Baztán using public transport?
A: Nuevo Baztán is part of the broader CiclaMadrid network, which connects various towns around Madrid, including a stage from Villarejo de Salvanés to Nuevo Baztán and another from Nuevo Baztán to Alcalá de Henares. Public transport options, such as regional buses, may allow bikes, but it's advisable to check with the specific transport provider for their policies on carrying bicycles before planning your trip.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Nuevo Baztán?
A: The komoot community highly rates the touring cycling experience in Nuevo Baztán, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse scenery, which includes the unique Alcarria landscape, river valleys, and historic towns. The variety of routes, from moderate to challenging, also receives positive feedback, allowing riders of all levels to find suitable options.
tips: It is a beautiful town, extraordinarily well cared for. Its steep streets overlook Arroyo de Vega from a hillside. Formerly called Olmeda de Cebolla, it changed its name in 1953. In its streets you will find whitewashed houses, fountains and a most picturesque setting. I advise you to look out at the viewpoint next to the church for some beautiful views.
This Guadalajara wine town has the ruins of one of the first Renaissance convents in Spain, that of San Antonio.
Country road in very good condition with a climb of 8 km having a very regular incline of 2%

This small town of about 600 inhabitants has a lot of history behind it. Its name probably comes from the Latin "ambitus" which indicated a fenced place. In the old station there is a perfect bar for a stop on the route.
Old laundry room. Now in use as a library.
Centennial elm tree in very good condition, it is located in the urban area, almost heading south, next to the gas station

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this gravel cycling route?
faqs.answer: This challenging gravel cycling route is estimated to take approximately 3 hours and 40 minutes to complete. However, actual duration can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and the specific conditions of the unpaved sections.

faqs.question: Is this gravel cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and is designed for cyclists with very good physical fitness. The terrain includes significant elevation changes and demanding unpaved gravel segments, which may require advanced riding skills and potentially even dismounting and pushing your bike in some parts. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Río Tajuña circular gravel route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is primarily gravel, with sections that are unpaved and demanding. While parts of the broader Tajuña Greenway use smoother, compacted surfaces, this specific circular route incorporates more challenging segments, including artificial gravel between Ambite and Mondéjar. Expect varied surfaces that contribute to its 'difficult' rating.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best seasons for cycling this route. The weather is typically milder, making the challenging terrain more enjoyable. Summers can be very hot in this region, and some slopes are drier and more barren, which might make cycling less comfortable during peak heat.

faqs.question: What notable sights and landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery and passes by several interesting points. You'll experience the rural heart of the region, including fertile riverine landscapes and olive groves. Highlights include the village of Olmeda de las Fuentes, the Ruins of the Convent of San Antonio, Mondéjar, and the Tajuña Greenway itself. You'll also pass the Old Ambite Station, the San Isidro Fountain, and the Centenary Elm of Nuevo Baztán.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it uses parts of the Vía Verde del Tajuña, which is a significant greenway in the region. You will also encounter segments of the Senda de Valmores and the Ruta Circular: Sector Este R-C Circular.

faqs.question: Is the Río Tajuña circular gravel route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the general area is popular for outdoor activities, the difficulty and specific terrain of this gravel cycling route make it less ideal for dogs, especially if they are running alongside a bike. If you do bring a dog, ensure they are well-conditioned for long distances and challenging surfaces, and always keep them on a leash, especially near villages or other trail users.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in La Alcarria region?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails like this one in the La Alcarria region. However, always respect private property, follow local regulations, and be mindful of any signage regarding protected areas or specific land use rules.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this circular route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route starting and ending in Nuevo Baztán, you can typically find parking within or near the town to begin your ride. Look for public parking areas in Nuevo Baztán, which serves as the primary access point for this loop.

faqs.question: What kind of natural scenery and wildlife can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The route traverses diverse landscapes characteristic of the Tajuña River valley. You'll cycle through fertile and lush riverine areas, abundant with poplars and olive groves, which contrast with the drier, more barren surrounding slopes. The blend of river valleys, scrubland, and dryland farming offers a varied visual experience. Keep an eye out for local birdlife and small mammals common to the region.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to get refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: The route passes through or near villages like Olmeda de las Fuentes and Mondéjar. These towns offer opportunities to experience local culture and cuisine, so you should be able to find cafes, bars, or small shops for refreshments and food, particularly in Mondéjar.

waypoints.name: Ruins of the Convent of San Antonio, Mondéjar
